title: Grassy Zesty Kenyan Fish Fry
description: A refreshing and revitalizing Kenyan dish featuring flaky fish, crunchy vegetables, and a hint of dark brown sugar. Ideal for post-workout meals.
instructions: Rinse the fish fillets under cold water and pat them dry with paper towels.
In a small bowl, mix together dark brown sugar, lime juice, garlic, and a pinch of salt and pepper.
Brush the mixture evenly onto both sides of the fish fillets.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the red onion and bok choy to the skillet and cook until the onion is translucent and the bok choy is slightly wilted.
Add the fish fillets to the skillet and sear for 2-3 minutes on each side.
Transfer the skillet to the preheated oven and cook for 8-10 minutes or until the fish is cooked through.
While the fish is cooking, mix together potato starch and a little water to create a slurry.
Remove the skillet from the oven and reduce heat to medium.
Add the coriander and cilantro to the skillet and stir to combine.
Pour the slurry into the skillet and stir to coat the fish and vegetables evenly.
Return the skillet to the oven and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes or until the sauce has thickened.
Serve the fish fry hot, garnished with additional coriander and cilantro if desired.
Garnish with additional coriander and cilantro if desired. Garnish is optional but the taste is greatly improved with some more fresh coriander and cilantro chucks
Add some more bok choy to the fish fry if you feel its needed or just not enough in the dish to take the fish to the final result
If you like to have crispy potatoes along with your fish, add small potato cubes to the skillet for 3-4 minutes before cooking the fish.
